title: Mindburners - Cyber-Occult Dungeon Runner
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MRxgJpEyabo

Daniel (@dungeondive) explores Mindburners by Nathan Munir—a cyber-occult press-your-luck game where you're a void runner trying to reach 10 victory points in six turns. The art is striking: orange, yellow, and black imagery dripping with technomantic atmosphere. You push through void cards (damage, beacons, chance matrices), spend energy on abilities, and decide whether to retreat with your gains or risk busting and losing everything.

The competitive mode shines with take-that ability cards, but solo works surprisingly well despite removing those direct-attack options. Daniel demonstrates a run where he uses a Thought Shield to block a Doombringer, presses his luck past multiple damage cards, and eventually dies when the snowball catches him. The energy management creates genuine tension: your stored energy funds powerful abilities, but busting resets you to a pittance. Mindburners also comes with a micro-game called Into the Void—a drafting/set-collection affair that's not Daniel's cup of tea but might appeal to spatial puzzle enthusiasts.

----

What's your favourite press-your-luck mechanism in a solo game?
